title: KEYS command for batch invalidation — blocks Redis for entire scan
expected_feedback: Standard: Invalidation strategy relies on KEYS pattern-matching, which scans the entire keyspace (5M keys) in a single blocking O(N) operation — Redis cannot serve other requests during the scan. This is a fundamentally broken invalidation approach that causes latency spikes for all clients. Even SCAN (the non-blocking cursor alternative) adds 10-100ms of unpredictable latency per iteration and is not suitable for real-time invalidation in hot paths. Fix: redesign so invalidation targets exact known keys — maintain an auxiliary Redis Set tracking related keys (SADD on write, SMEMBERS+DEL on invalidate), use Hash structure where one DEL removes all fields, or adopt structured key naming with explicit DEL of deterministic key lists.
